register.note: No opening visual — the top of the page is a text masthead plus metadata card, where ciechanowski-watch/00 anchors with a full-width watch render.
No hero to carry the mechanism, unlike seeing-theory/01 whose opening plot shows the residual squares themselves.

The See-it surface self-signals (axis labels, '@ start', 'the valley floor (OLS)', the labeled metric strip) like distill-momentum/02, short of 4 because its captions are less tightly bound per-figure.

The prior aliasing is fixed — continuous ~1.5px anti-aliased contours and smooth band boundaries on a Distill-soft red bowl with dead air closed by the metric strip; below 4 only on two-column (not single-protagonist) composition and a label/tick micro-nit vs ciechanowski-watch/02.

The descent renders as discrete steppable iterates with a learning-rate control, explanatory not decorative like 3b1b-gradient-descent/02; held at 3 since easing/smoothness can't be confirmed from stills.
Grammar holds — red = error/height (not an alarm wash), periwinkle = descent agent, blue = steps, AA prose — as disciplined as r2d3-trees/00; below 4 because the steepest red corner nears flattening.

hero.note: A hero would need the now-clean See-it bowl promoted to a full-bleed, thumbnail-legible opening that shows the OLS valley and one descent path.

assessment.note: Explain shows a recall MCQ stack with a persistent Raw/Standardised bowl, but the closing transfer item is not visibly an open/interactive prompt.

blocking: Add a full-width hero specimen (the See-it bowl) so hero-as-protagonist and mechanism-in-the-picture stop being forced 0.
Make the closing transfer item interactive or open rather than a fifth recall MCQ.
verdict.summary: Atmosphere-finish now clears its floor at 3 (render aliasing genuinely fixed, dead air closed); the exhibit holds only on the absent hero, which forces two register dimensions to 0.
